svg.title: MCP architecture diagram
svg.desc: Shows how an AI host (Claude) connects through MCP clients to multiple MCP servers, which in turn expose tools, resources, and prompts from real-world services like file systems, databases, GitHub, and Slack.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does an MCP client do?')
g.text: manages connections · routes calls · handles auth

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What can an MCP file system server do?')
sendPrompt('What can an MCP database server do?')
sendPrompt('What can an MCP GitHub server do?')
sendPrompt('What can an MCP Slack server do?')

svg.text: Each MCP server exposes three primitive types

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What are MCP tools and how are they called?')
sendPrompt('How does MCP transport work — stdio vs SSE?')
g.text: stdio (local process) or HTTP + SSE (remote server)
